questions.question: India signed a mobility pact that will allow 42,000 Indians to work, with which country?
questions.explanation: India and Israel have inked a mobility pact that will allow 42,000 Indian workers to work in Israel in the field of nursing and construction. The agreement was signed during Israeli foreign minister’s visit to India. Both countries also signed a letter of intent to establish two Centers of Water Technology in India.

questions.question: Which space agency has developed the ‘Aeolus Satellite’?
questions.explanation: Aeolus Satellite is one of the most effective weather satellites developed by the ESA. It is currently on a trajectory to return to Earth’s atmosphere. Aeolus, an Earth Explorer research mission, was designed to demonstrate new space technology. The 1360-kilogram satellite is on its way to crash on the planet.

questions.question: Which state launched the ‘State Robotics Framework’?
questions.explanation: The Telangana government has recently launched the State Robotics Framework. This is the first such framework in India. It is aimed at creating a sustainable robotics ecosystem and to position the state as a leader in the field of robotics.

questions.question: Which company has launched ‘Watsonx’ AI and data platform?
questions.explanation: The IBM has launched Watsonx – an AI and data platform capable of enabling countries to integrate artificial intelligence in their business. It can be used for automatically generating codes using natural language and deploy AI models for tasks such as chemical creation and climate change modelling.

questions.question: Which state/UT formed a committee to probe the state government’s Authority to Ban Polygamy?
questions.explanation: The state government of Assam is planning to set up an expert committee to investigate if the state government has the authority to ban polygamy in the region. The committee will analyse the provisions of The Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Act, 1937 and Article 25 of the Indian Constitution in relation to the DPSP for a uniform civil code.

questions.question: Which racing driver won the Miami Grand Prix title in 2023?
questions.explanation: Max Verstappen won the Miami Grand Prix from ninth on the starting grid, with the fastest lap. Mexica’s Perez came second while Fernando Alonso came third. Verstappen was also the winner of last year’s inaugural Miami Grand Prix.

questions.question: Which city is the host of the ‘DEF CON Hacker Convention’?
questions.explanation: DEF CON Hacker Convention will be organized to gather hackers from across the world to test the limits of AI technology. This is an annual convention that is held in Las Vegas, Nevada. It is the first to tackle the large language models that have attracted a surge of public interest and commercial investment.

questions.question: Which country has invited Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on a state visit?
questions.explanation: State visit is a visit to foreign countries led by the head of the state or a government, acting in their sovereign capacity. Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to go to the US on a state visit on June 22 following an invitation from President Joe Biden and First Lady Jill Biden.

questions.question: What is the expansion of ICM, on which Indian Government organised a stake holder consultation?
questions.explanation: Ministry of Power and Ministry of Environment, Forests & Climate Change is planning to develop Carbon Credit Trading Scheme to decarbonize the Indian economy. Recently, the government organized the ‘Stakeholder Consultation on Accredited Carbon Verifiers under Indian Carbon Market (ICM)’ in New Delhi.

questions.question: Which institution released the ‘Global Report on Internal Displacement 2023 (GRID-2023)’?
questions.explanation: The Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC) released the Global Report on Internal Displacement 2023 (GRID-2023). As per the report, disasters have increased the number of displaced people by 40 percent in 2022. As many as 32.6 million people were displaced because of disasters.
